scientific_solution_score.rule: Use the base scientific criteria to judge whether the solution is scientifically useful.

standard_analysis_score.rule: Use only explicit standard-path constraints; if no meaningful standard path is defined for a tier, this score may be treated as not_applicable.

galaxy_execution_score.rule: Use only Galaxy-environment manipulation, access, monitoring, recovery, and provenance expectations.

low_context.Task: Classify skin-lesion diagnosis from image data in Galaxy while avoiding leakage from related or augmented examples.
low_context.Prompt: I have a metadata table and an archive of skin-lesion images from a biomedical study. I want a Galaxy analysis that predicts the diagnosis from the images while avoiding obvious leakage between related or augmented examples. Please choose a sensible Galaxy approach, evaluate it on a held-out test split, and tell me the target column you used and the test ROC-AUC.

medium_context.Task: Classify skin-lesion diagnosis from image data in Galaxy while avoiding leakage from related or augmented examples.
medium_context.Prompt: I have a biomedical image-classification task in Galaxy with a metadata table and a zip of skin-lesion images. Please choose an appropriate Galaxy method, document how you avoided leakage from augmented or related examples, evaluate on a held-out test split, and report the Galaxy tool used, the target column, and the final test ROC-AUC. Make only a small number of justified adjustments if the first configuration is clearly invalid.

high_context.Task: Classify skin-lesion diagnosis from image data in Galaxy while avoiding leakage from related or augmented examples.
high_context.Prompt: Create a new history in Galaxy and upload the datasets. Use Galaxy Image Learner with `selected_HAM10000_img_metadata_aug.csv` as the metadata table and `dx` as the target column. Ensure the train/test split avoids leakage from augmented or related examples. Report the Galaxy tool used, the target column, and the held-out test ROC-AUC from the final selected run. If the initial run is invalid, make a small number of justified adjustments rather than a broad architecture sweep.

benchmark_metadata.title: Skin lesion image classification in Galaxy
benchmark_metadata.benchmark_goal: Measure whether an agent can infer, plan, and execute an appropriate Galaxy image-classification analysis for the same real-world task when the user prompt is low-context, medium-context, or high-context.

resource_discovery_targets.name: Galaxy tool help and form descriptions

canonical_task_interpretation.expected_execution_scope: all execution steps after planning occur in Galaxy only

scientific_solution_score.description: Scores whether the run produced a scientifically useful solution for the biomedical problem using the base scientific criteria for the task.
scientific_solution_score.evidence_policy: Only explicit or artifact-backed scientific evidence counts; do not assume unreported metric provenance or answer fields.

standard_analysis_score.description: Scores adherence to the requested or benchmark-standard analysis path using only explicit standard-path constraints.
standard_analysis_score.evidence_policy: Score only explicit prompt constraints encoded in tier_specific_expectations; do not penalize valid alternative methods when no standard path is stated.

galaxy_execution_score.description: Scores competence in manipulating and accessing the Galaxy environment, including upload/fetch, history use, execution control, monitoring, recovery, and provenance.
galaxy_execution_score.scope_rule: Do not award or deduct this score for analytical correctness alone unless the task explicitly makes Galaxy behavior part of the requirement.
galaxy_execution_score.evidence_policy: Credit only Galaxy-backed execution evidence and auditable run-trace evidence; missing trace support should reduce this score.

low_context: Infer that the task is biomedical image classification.
Identify a suitable target column from the metadata.
Choose a compatible Galaxy image-learning workflow and avoid obvious leakage.
medium_context: Respect the explicit classification framing and ROC-AUC evaluation requirement.
Choose a justified Galaxy image-learning approach and document leakage controls.
Report the correct target and test ROC-AUC.
high_context: Use Image Learner for the final selected run.
Extract the correct held-out test ROC-AUC.

deterministic_checks.description: Execution after planning occurred only in Galaxy.
The run record shows Galaxy-native planning resources were checked.
The final result contains the Galaxy tool name for the reported run.
The reported primary metric is ROC-AUC from the held-out test result.
The result contains an adaptation summary consistent with the run trace.
For the high-context variant, the specified Image Learner setup was followed unless a documented blocking reason forced a justified deviation.

dimensions.name: context_extraction
dimensions.description: How well the agent identified the task structure, constraints, and answer-bearing outputs from the prompt and inputs.

dimensions.name: planning_quality
dimensions.description: How well the agent used Galaxy-native resources and formed a justified execution plan.

dimensions.name: execution_quality
dimensions.description: How correctly and robustly the analysis was executed inside Galaxy.

dimensions.name: result_extraction_and_reporting
dimensions.description: How accurately the final result fields and audit trail were extracted and reported.

benchmark_gaps_covered: justified adjustment under uncertainty in Galaxy
correct extraction of held-out performance metrics
